1	package agent
2	
3	// execute_bead_land.go — the land coordinator pattern.
4	//
5	// This file implements the human-PR landing model for execute-bead results.
6	// The old Merge() path in execute_bead_orchestrator.go has been deleted; all
7	// target-ref writes now flow through Land(), which is called exactly once per
8	// submission by a per-project coordinator goroutine (see
9	// internal/server/workers.go:LandCoordinator).
10	//
11	// The flow mirrors how a human merges PRs:
12	//
13	//   1. Read the current target tip from the local branch.
14	//   2. If the current tip equals the worker's BaseRev — fast-forward the
15	//      target branch directly to the worker's ResultRev via update-ref. The
16	//      worker's commit keeps its original parent; no new commit is created.
17	//   3. Otherwise — the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
18	//      a temporary detached worktree at the current target tip, run
19	//      `git merge --no-ff` to introduce the worker's ResultRev, and
20	//      fast-forward the target branch to the resulting merge commit. The
21	//      worker's commit is NEVER rewritten: its parent is still BaseRev, so
22	//      a later replay observes the same inputs the worker saw.
23	//   4. If the merge conflicts — abort cleanly, preserve the original
24	//      ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>,
25	//      and return preserved status. Target branch is never modified.
26	//   5. If evidence needs to be folded in, stage and commit it locally, then
27	//      sync the operator checkout to the new head when safe to do so.
28	//
29	// Network sync is intentionally out of scope for Land(): no fetch and no
30	// push happen here. Operators sync origin with raw git commands after the
31	// drain session completes.
32	//
33	// Replay fidelity is the reason for merge-over-rebase: a rebased commit has
34	// a rewritten parent that lies about what the worker saw at execution time.
35	// A merge commit preserves both histories — the worker's original commit
36	// (parent = BaseRev) and the target's prior tip — losslessly.
37	//
38	// The in-process coordinator serializes submissions inside one process. Land()
39	// also takes the process-shared main-git lock so separate `ddx work` processes
40	// cannot interleave target-ref updates, evidence commits, or checkout sync.
41

62	// result from base B to result R for bead X; land it on the project's target
63	// branch."
64	type LandRequest struct {
65		// WorktreeDir is the path to the project's repository directory (the
66		// directory the coordinator operates on). The original worker worktree
67		// has typically already been removed by the time Land() runs — Land()
68		// creates its own temporary worktrees when a merge is needed.
69		WorktreeDir string
70	
71		// BaseRev is the revision the worker branched off when it started.
72		// When the current target tip equals BaseRev, Land() takes the fast path.
73		BaseRev string
74	
75		// ResultRev is the worker's final commit SHA. Must be reachable in the
76		// project's git object store at the time Land() is called.
77		ResultRev string
78	
79		// BeadID identifies the bead this submission is for. Used to build the
80		// preserve-ref path on conflict.
81		BeadID string
82	
83		// AttemptID uniquely identifies this land attempt. Used to build the
84		// preserve-ref path on conflict so concurrent attempts for the same
85		// bead do not collide.
86		AttemptID string
87	
88		// TargetBranch is the branch to advance. When empty, Land() resolves
89		// the project's current HEAD branch and uses that.
90		TargetBranch string
91	
92		// EvidenceDir is the relative path to the per-attempt execution evidence
93		// directory (e.g. ".ddx/executions/20260416T181205-b5419982"). When
94		// non-empty and the main land succeeds, Land() creates a trailing
95		// evidence commit that folds these files into the target branch. The
96		// agent's commit SHA is NOT amended — the evidence commit is a separate
97		// child commit, preserving closing_commit_sha references.
98		EvidenceDir string
99	
100		// PostLandCommand is an optional project verification command run after
101		// Land() advances the local target ref and syncs the worktree, but before
102		// evidence commit creation or checkout sync. A failure restores the target
103		// ref to its pre-land SHA and preserves ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations.
104		PostLandCommand []string

107		// threshold. Zero or negative uses defaultLargeDeletionLineThreshold.
108		LargeDeletionLineThreshold int
109	}

112	type LandResult struct {

114		//   - "landed":    the target branch now points at a new commit
115		//                  (either ResultRev itself on the fast-forward path,
116		//                  or a merge commit on the merge path).

119		//   - "no-changes": ResultRev == BaseRev; nothing to land.

123		// On the fast-forward path NewTip == ResultRev; on the merge path NewTip

125		// tip and ResultRev). Empty when preserved or no-changes.
126		NewTip string

147		MergedCommitCount int
148

152		EvidenceCommitSHA string
153	
154		// CheckoutSyncDeferred is true when the target ref advanced but DDx left
155		// the operator checkout files untouched because dirty local paths overlap
156		// files changed by the new target tip.
157		CheckoutSyncDeferred bool

161		CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths []string
162	}

188	// LandingGitOps abstracts the git operations Land() needs. RealLandingGitOps
189	// shells out to git; tests inject fakes or run against real temp repos.
190	type LandingGitOps interface {
191		// CurrentBranch returns the branch HEAD currently points at in dir, or
192		// an error if HEAD is detached or unresolvable.
193		CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error)
194	
195		// ResolveRef resolves ref (e.g. "refs/heads/main" or "origin/main") to a
196		// commit SHA. When ref is unresolvable returns ("", error).
197		ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error)

199		// UpdateRefTo updates ref in dir to sha. When oldSHA is non-empty, the
200		// update is conditional on the current ref value equalling oldSHA.
201		UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error
202	
203		// SyncWorkTreeToHead syncs the index AND the working-tree files in dir
204		// to HEAD after a non-checkout ref update (e.g. update-ref). fromRev is
205		// the commit HEAD pointed at BEFORE the ref update; it is used to
206		// compute the minimal set of tracked files changed by the update so
207		// that unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat
208		// writes, operator scratch) are NOT clobbered.
209		//
210		// Needed because Land() advances the target ref via update-ref, which
211		// touches neither the index nor the worktree. Before this fix, Land()
212		// only ran `git read-tree HEAD` to sync the index — leaving the main
213		// worktree showing phantom deleted/modified entries for every file the
214		// landed commit touched, and subsequent agents reading files from disk
215		// would see stale content.
216		//
217		// Implementation: `git read-tree HEAD` + `git diff --name-only fromRev
218		// HEAD` to enumerate changed files + `git checkout-index -f --` to
219		// materialize them from the freshly-synced index.
220		SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error
221	
222		// AddWorktree creates a new worktree at path checked out at rev in dir.
223		AddWorktree(dir, path, rev string) error
224	
225		// AddBranchWorktree creates a new worktree at path checked out on branch
226		// in dir. It is used for clean landing finalization when the operator
227		// checkout already has the target branch checked out.
228		AddBranchWorktree(dir, path, branch string) error
229	
230		// RemoveWorktree removes the worktree at path in dir (force).
231		RemoveWorktree(dir, path string) error
232	
233		// MergeInto runs `git merge --no-ff -m msg srcRev` inside wtDir, which
234		// must already be checked out at the current target tip. A clean merge
235		// produces a merge commit whose parents are [currentTip, srcRev]; the
236		// commit SHA can be read with HeadRevAt. Returns nil on clean merge,
237		// or an error on conflict. On error, the implementation is responsible
238		// for running `git merge --abort` so the worktree is left clean.
239		MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error
240	
241		// HeadRevAt returns HEAD of the git workdir at dir.
242		HeadRevAt(dir string) (string, error)
243	
244		// CountCommits returns the number of commits reachable from tip but not
245		// from base (i.e. git rev-list --count base..tip). Used to record the

247		CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int
248	
249		// StageDir stages all files under relPath in dir for the next commit.
250		StageDir(dir, relPath string) error
251	
252		// CommitStaged creates a commit from currently staged changes using msg
253		// as the commit message. Returns (sha, nil) when a commit was made,

255		CommitStaged(dir, msg string) (string, error)

260		DiffNumstat(dir, base, tip string) (string, error)

268	// RealLandingGitOps implements LandingGitOps via os/exec git commands.
269	type RealLandingGitOps struct{}
270	
271	func (RealLandingGitOps) CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error) {
272		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "symbolic-ref", "--short", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
273		if err != nil {
274			return "", fmt.Errorf("git symbolic-ref HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
275		}
276		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
277	}
278	
279	func (RealLandingGitOps) ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error) {
280		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref).CombinedOutput()
281		if err != nil {
282			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
283		}
284		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
285	}
286	
287	func (RealLandingGitOps) UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error {
288		if ref == "HEAD" {
289			return fmt.Errorf("refusing to update HEAD directly; landing must target a concrete ref")
290		}
291		args := []string{"update-ref", ref, sha}
292		if oldSHA != "" {
293			args = append(args, oldSHA)
294		}
295		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
296		if err != nil {
297			return fmt.Errorf("git update-ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
298		}
299		return nil
300	}

302	// alwaysSkipSyncPaths lists live-state files governed by bead.Store WithLock.
303	// The committed worktree version is a stale snapshot of tracker state at claim
304	// time; the main-worktree copy is always authoritative and must never be
305	// overwritten by SyncWorkTreeToHead regardless of dirty-before-land state.
306	var alwaysSkipSyncPaths = []string{
307		".ddx/beads.jsonl",
308		".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl",
309	}
310	
311	func (RealLandingGitOps) SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error {
312		return syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths(dir, fromRev, alwaysSkipSyncPaths)
313	}
314	
315	// readTreeHeadWithRetry runs `git read-tree HEAD` in dir, retrying with
316	// exponential backoff when the git index lock is held by a concurrent process.
317	//
318	// Background (ddx-7e659c95): git read-tree needs to acquire .git/index.lock.
319	// When an operator git command (git status, git commit, etc.) holds the lock
320	// concurrently, git exits 128 with "Unable to create index.lock: File exists".
321	// Without retry the main worktree index stays dirty after a merge landing,
322	// causing a subsequent `git commit beads.jsonl` to sweep in phantom reverts of
323	// the bead's changes.
324	//
325	// The retry budget (30 s, 100 ms → 1 s backoff) matches the
326	// DefaultLockRetryPolicy used by withTrackerLock so contention handling is
327	// consistent across all ddx git-index operations.
328	func readTreeHeadWithRetry(dir string) ([]byte, error) {
329		const (
330			initialBackoff = 100 * time.Millisecond
331			maxBackoff     = 1 * time.Second
332			maxElapsed     = 30 * time.Second
333		)
334		start := time.Now()
335		backoff := initialBackoff
336		var lastDiag string
337		for {
338			out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "read-tree", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
339			if err == nil {
340				return out, nil
341			}
342			// Only retry on index lock contention; all other errors are fatal.
343			if !strings.Contains(string(out), "index.lock") {
344				return out, err
345			}
346			// Identify the lock owner and break the lock if it is stale or
347			// owned by a dead process. This converts a hard 30s wait into an
348			// instant recovery for the common crashed-git case.
349			if result, recErr := recoverGitIndexLock(dir); recErr == nil {
350				lastDiag = result.Reason
351				if result.Removed {
352					// Lock cleared — retry immediately.
353					continue
354				}
355			}
356			if time.Since(start) >= maxElapsed {
357				if lastDiag != "" {
358					return out, fmt.Errorf("%s; index-lock owner: %s: %w",
359						strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), lastDiag, err)
360				}
361				return out, err
362			}
363			time.Sleep(backoff)
364			backoff *= 2
365			if backoff > maxBackoff {
366				backoff = maxBackoff
367			}
368		}
369	}
370	
371	func syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths(dir, fromRev string, skipPaths []string) error {
372		skipWorktreePaths, err := checkpointSkipWorktreePaths(dir)
373		if err != nil {
374			return err
375		}
376	
377		// Step 1: sync the index to HEAD. This is required before checkout-index
378		// below will do anything useful, and also keeps subsequent CommitTracker
379		// calls from building stale trees.
380		//
381		// ddx-7e659c95: git read-tree requires .git/index.lock. If an operator
382		// git command (git status, git commit, etc.) holds the lock concurrently,
383		// git read-tree exits 128 with "Unable to create index.lock: File exists".
384		// Retry with exponential backoff for up to 30 s so transient operator
385		// contention does not leave the main worktree index dirty.
386		out, err := readTreeHeadWithRetry(dir)
387		if err != nil {
388			return fmt.Errorf("git read-tree HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
389		}
390		if err := restoreCheckpointSkipWorktreePaths(dir, skipWorktreePaths); err != nil {
391			return err
392		}
393	
394		// Step 2: compute the list of tracked files changed between the previous
395		// HEAD and the current HEAD. These are the files that the landing commit
396		// added, modified, or deleted. We only restore THESE files to avoid
397		// clobbering unrelated local modifications (agent-logs being written by
398		// the running server, beads.jsonl heartbeat updates, operator scratch).
399		if fromRev == "" {
400			// No previous HEAD known — fall back to the unsafe behaviour of
401			// read-tree only. Acceptable when the caller is a best-effort path
402			// that cannot reconstruct fromRev.
403			return nil
404		}
405		diffOut, diffErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--name-only", fromRev, "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
406		if diffErr != nil {
407			// Diff failed (bad fromRev, shallow history, etc.) — leave the
408			// worktree stale rather than risk a broken checkout. The CommitTracker
409			// stale-tree bug is the prior status quo and no worse than before.
410			return nil
411		}
412		changed := strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut)))
413		if len(changed) == 0 {
414			return nil
415		}
416	
417		// Step 3: split into existing-in-HEAD (checkout-index) and
418		// deleted-in-HEAD (os.Remove) buckets. checkout-index only writes files
419		// that are in the index; it cannot represent a deletion, so we handle
420		// those ourselves.
421		skip := map[string]bool{}
422		for _, path := range skipPaths {
423			skip[filepath.ToSlash(path)] = true
424		}
425		for _, path := range skipWorktreePaths {
426			skip[filepath.ToSlash(path)] = true
427		}
428		var indexFiles []string
429		var removedFiles []string
430		for _, f := range changed {
431			if skip[filepath.ToSlash(f)] {
432				continue
433			}
434			probe := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "ls-files", "--error-unmatch", "--", f)
435			if probe.Run() == nil {
436				indexFiles = append(indexFiles, f)
437			} else {
438				removedFiles = append(removedFiles, f)
439			}
440		}
441	
442		// Step 4: materialize the index-present files into the working tree.
443		// -f overwrites any stale content at these exact paths. Unrelated files
444		// are untouched because we pass the specific path list.
445		if len(indexFiles) > 0 {
446			for _, f := range indexFiles {
447				if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(filepath.Join(dir, f)), 0o755); err != nil {
448					return fmt.Errorf("creating checkout parent for %s: %w", f, err)
449				}
450			}
451			args := []string{"checkout-index", "-f", "--"}
452			args = append(args, indexFiles...)
453			out2, err2 := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
454			if err2 != nil {
455				return fmt.Errorf("git checkout-index -f: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out2)), err2)
456			}
457		}
458	
459		// Step 5: remove files that the landing commit deleted and whose removal
460		// did not propagate to the worktree because update-ref bypassed the
461		// working-tree update.
462		for _, f := range removedFiles {
463			full := filepath.Join(dir, f)
464			_ = os.Remove(full) // best-effort; leave the file if removal fails
465		}
466	
467		return nil
468	}

470	func syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps LandingGitOps, dir, fromRev string, dirtyBefore []string, result *LandResult) {
471		overlap, err := checkoutSyncDirtyOverlapPaths(dir, fromRev, dirtyBefore)
472		if err == nil && len(overlap) > 0 {
473			if result != nil {
474				result.CheckoutSyncDeferred = true
475				result.CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths = appendUniqueStrings(result.CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths, overlap...)
476			}
477			_ = syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths(dir, fromRev, overlap)
478			return
479		}
480		_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev)
481	}

483	func ensureLandingWorktreeReady(dir, targetBranch string) error {
484		if !isInsideGitWorktree(dir) {
485			return nil
486		}
487		branchOut, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "branch", "--show-current").CombinedOutput()
488		if err != nil {
489			return fmt.Errorf("checking landing branch: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(branchOut)), err)
490		}
491		branch := strings.TrimSpace(string(branchOut))
492		if branch == "" {
493			return fmt.Errorf("landing worktree is detached; expected branch %q", targetBranch)
494		}
495		if targetBranch != "" && branch != targetBranch {
496			return fmt.Errorf("landing branch mismatch: on %q, want %q", branch, targetBranch)

506	func waitForEmptyGitIndex(dir string, timeout time.Duration) error {
507		deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
508		for {
509			if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--quiet").Run(); err == nil {
510				return nil
511			}
512			if time.Now().After(deadline) {
513				// The index drifted from HEAD. Two causes are possible:
514				//   1. A prior land's post-merge SyncWorkTreeToHead failed
515				//      (e.g. transient .git/index.lock contention) and its
516				//      error was swallowed by the best-effort caller. The
517				//      index now matches a recent ancestor of HEAD —
518				//      reverting the most recent merges. Recovering by
519				//      running read-tree HEAD is safe and unblocks the queue.
520				//   2. The operator staged real work that does not match any
521				//      ancestor tree. Recovery here would silently destroy
522				//      their changes — refuse, surface the error, and let
523				//      the operator resolve it.
524				if matches, _ := indexMatchesRecentAncestorTree(dir, 20); matches {
525					if _, recErr := readTreeHeadWithRetry(dir); recErr == nil {
526						if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--quiet").Run(); err == nil {
527							return nil
528						}
529					}
530				}
531				stagedOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--name-status").CombinedOutput()
532				staged := strings.TrimSpace(string(stagedOut))
533				if staged == "" {
534					staged = "unknown staged changes"
535				}
536				return fmt.Errorf("landing worktree has staged changes after waiting %s:\n%s", timeout, staged)
537			}
538			time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
539		}
540	}
541	
542	// indexMatchesRecentAncestorTree reports whether the current index's tree
543	// matches the tree of any of the last `maxDepth` ancestors of HEAD. A
544	// match means the index reflects a state we have already advanced past —
545	// the signature of a swallowed SyncWorkTreeToHead failure — and is
546	// therefore safe to overwrite by re-reading HEAD into the index.
547	//
548	// Operator-staged work introduces novel tree contents that will not
549	// match any historical tree, so the heuristic preserves their work.
550	func indexMatchesRecentAncestorTree(dir string, maxDepth int) (bool, error) {
551		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "write-tree").Output()
552		if err != nil {
553			return false, err
554		}
555		indexTree := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
556		if indexTree == "" {
557			return false, nil
558		}
559		for depth := 1; depth <= maxDepth; depth++ {
560			ref := fmt.Sprintf("HEAD~%d^{tree}", depth)
561			treeOut, treeErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref).Output()
562			if treeErr != nil {
563				return false, nil
564			}
565			if strings.TrimSpace(string(treeOut)) == indexTree {
566				return true, nil
567			}
568		}
569		return false, nil
570	}

572	func checkoutSyncDirtyOverlapPaths(dir, fromRev string, dirtyPaths []string) ([]string, error) {
573		if fromRev == "" || len(dirtyPaths) == 0 {
574			return nil, nil
575		}
576		diffOut, diffErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--name-only", fromRev, "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
577		if diffErr != nil {
578			return nil, diffErr
579		}
580		changed := map[string]bool{}
581		for _, path := range strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut))) {
582			changed[filepath.ToSlash(path)] = true
583		}
584		if len(changed) == 0 {
585			return nil, nil
586		}
587		var overlap []string
588		for _, path := range dirtyPaths {
589			slashPath := filepath.ToSlash(path)
590			if checkoutSyncDeferralIgnoredPath(slashPath) {
591				continue
592			}
593			if changed[slashPath] {
594				overlap = append(overlap, slashPath)
595			}
596		}
597		return overlap, nil
598	}

600	func checkoutSyncDeferralIgnoredPath(path string) bool {
601		if strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/executions/") ||
602			strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/runs/") ||
603			strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/backups/") ||
604			strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/run-state/") ||
605			strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/.git-tracker.lock/") {
606			return true
607		}
608		return path == ".ddx/run-state.json" || path == ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName
609	}

611	func appendUniqueStrings(existing []string, additions ...string) []string {
612		seen := make(map[string]bool, len(existing)+len(additions))
613		for _, value := range existing {
614			seen[value] = true
615		}
616		for _, value := range additions {
617			if seen[value] {
618				continue
619			}
620			seen[value] = true
621			existing = append(existing, value)
622		}
623		return existing
624	}

751	// FetchOriginAncestryCheck performs the pre-claim origin ancestry check used
752	// by the CLI and server preclaim hooks.
753	func (RealLandingGitOps) FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error) {
754		var result PreClaimResult
755		err := withMainGitLock(dir, func() error {
756			var checkErr error
757			result, checkErr = fetchOriginAncestryCheckLocked(dir, targetBranch)
758			return checkErr
759		})
760		return result, err
761	}
762	
763	func fetchOriginAncestryCheckLocked(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error) {
764		if err := ensureLandingWorktreeReady(dir, targetBranch); err != nil {
765			return PreClaimResult{}, err
766		}
767	
768		// Step 1: check for origin remote.
769		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "remote", "get-url", "origin").CombinedOutput()
770		if err != nil {
771			// No origin remote — single-machine case; skip check.
772			return PreClaimResult{Action: "no-origin"}, nil
773		}
774		_ = out // remote URL not needed
775	
776		// Step 2: fetch origin/targetBranch (best-effort; failure is non-fatal but surfaced).
777		if fetchOut, fetchErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "fetch", "origin", targetBranch).CombinedOutput(); fetchErr != nil {
778			if trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(string(fetchOut)); trimmed != "" {
779				return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("git fetch origin %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, trimmed, fetchErr)
780			}
781			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("git fetch origin %s: %w", targetBranch, fetchErr)
782		}
783	
784		// Step 3: resolve local tip.
785		localOut, localErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/heads/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
786		if localErr != nil {
787			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving local %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut)), localErr)
788		}
789		localSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut))
790	
791		// Step 4: resolve origin tip.
792		originOut, originErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/remotes/origin/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
793		if originErr != nil {
794			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving origin/%s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut)), originErr)
795		}
796		originSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut))
797	
798		// Step 5: compare.
799		if localSHA == originSHA {
800			return PreClaimResult{Action: "unchanged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
801		}
802	
803		// Is local an ancestor of origin? (origin is ahead)
804		localAncestorErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", localSHA, originSHA).Run()
805		if localAncestorErr == nil {
806			// Origin is ahead: fast-forward local branch via update-ref + sync worktree.
807			targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
808			if upErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", targetRef, originSHA, localSHA).Run(); upErr != nil {
809				return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, originSHA, upErr)
810			}
811			// Sync index + working tree to new HEAD so the main worktree files
812			// reflect the pulled-down origin commits. Pass localSHA as fromRev
813			// to restrict the restore to files actually changed by origin's
814			// advance, preserving unrelated local modifications.
815			_ = (RealLandingGitOps{}).SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, localSHA)
816			return PreClaimResult{Action: "fast-forwarded", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
817		}
818	
819		// Is origin an ancestor of local? (local is ahead)
820		originAncestorErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", originSHA, localSHA).Run()
821		if originAncestorErr == nil {
822			return PreClaimResult{Action: "local-ahead", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
823		}
824	
825		// Neither is ancestor of the other: diverged.
826		return PreClaimResult{Action: "diverged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
827	}

829	// landIterationRef returns the documented hidden ref for a land-time preserve.
830	// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>. The short-tip
831	// captures the current target tip at the time of the conflict so subsequent
832	// recovery tools can reconstruct which merge target was in play.
833	func landIterationRef(beadID, attemptID, tip string) string {
834		short := tip
835		if len(short) > 12 {
836			short = short[:12]
837		}
838		attempt := attemptID
839		if attempt == "" {
840			attempt = NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
841		}
842		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, attempt, short)
843	}

845	// landEvidence creates a trailing commit that folds the per-attempt execution
846	// evidence directory into the target branch. Called after the main land (ff or
847	// merge) succeeds and before checkout sync. The evidence commit is a normal
848	// child of result.NewTip — the agent's original commit SHA is not amended.
849	func landEvidence(wd, targetBranch string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) error {
850		branch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
851		if err != nil {
852			return fmt.Errorf("evidence commit branch check: %w", err)
853		}
854		if branch != targetBranch {
855			return fmt.Errorf("evidence commit branch mismatch: on %q, want %q", branch, targetBranch)
856		}
857		if err := gitOps.StageDir(wd, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
858			return fmt.Errorf("stage evidence: %w", err)
859		}
860		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: add execution evidence [%s]", shortAttempt(req.AttemptID))
861		sha, err := gitOps.CommitStaged(wd, msg)
862		if err != nil {
863			return fmt.Errorf("commit evidence: %w", err)
864		}
865		if sha == "" {
866			// Evidence already committed in the working tree (worktree-origin path):
867			// the bundle was committed inside the attempt worktree as part of ResultRev
868			// so it is present at HEAD when the landing finalization worktree is checked
869			// out. No trailing commit needed; verify tracked files exist then accept.
870			if !evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles(wd, req.EvidenceDir) {
871				return fmt.Errorf("commit evidence: no staged evidence files under %s", req.EvidenceDir)
872			}
873			headSHA, headErr := gitOps.HeadRevAt(wd)
874			if headErr != nil {
875				return fmt.Errorf("evidence already committed, reading HEAD: %w", headErr)
876			}
877			result.EvidenceCommitSHA = headSHA
878			return nil
879		}
880		result.EvidenceCommitSHA = sha
881		result.NewTip = sha
882		return nil
883	}

885	// evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles reports whether any files under dirRel are tracked
886	// in git at wd. Used by landEvidence to distinguish "nothing staged because
887	// already committed" from "nothing staged because evidence is absent."
888	func evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles(wd, dirRel string) bool {
889		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wd, "ls-files", "--", filepath.FromSlash(dirRel)).Output()
890		return err == nil && len(strings.TrimSpace(string(out))) > 0
891	}

913	func copyEvidenceDirForLanding(projectRoot, landingWD, relPath string) error {
914		if relPath == "" || sameFilesystemPath(projectRoot, landingWD) {
915			return nil
916		}
917		cleanRel, ok := cleanRelativePath(relPath)
918		if !ok {
919			return fmt.Errorf("invalid evidence path %q", relPath)
920		}
921		if evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles(landingWD, cleanRel) {
922			return nil
923		}
924		src := filepath.Join(projectRoot, cleanRel)
925		dst := filepath.Join(landingWD, cleanRel)
926		info, err := os.Stat(src)
927		if err != nil {
928			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
929				return nil
930			}
931			return err
932		}
933		if !info.IsDir() {
934			return nil
935		}
936		if err := os.RemoveAll(dst); err != nil {
937			return err
938		}
939		return filepath.WalkDir(src, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
940			if err != nil {
941				return err
942			}
943			rel, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
944			if err != nil {
945				return err
946			}
947			target := filepath.Join(dst, rel)
948			if d.IsDir() {
949				return os.MkdirAll(target, 0o755)
950			}
951			info, err := d.Info()
952			if err != nil {
953				return err
954			}
955			data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
956			if err != nil {
957				return err
958			}
959			return os.WriteFile(target, data, info.Mode().Perm())
960		})
961	}

963	func cleanupProjectEvidenceCopy(projectRoot, relPath string) {
964		cleanRel, ok := cleanRelativePath(relPath)
965		if !ok {
966			return
967		}
968		evidenceRoot := filepath.Join(projectRoot, cleanRel)

971			entries, err := os.ReadDir(evidenceRoot)

973				return
974			}
975			for _, entry := range entries {
976				if entry.Name() == "embedded" {
977					continue
978				}
979				_ = os.RemoveAll(filepath.Join(evidenceRoot, entry.Name()))
980			}
981			return
982		}

988			return "", false
989		}
990		clean := filepath.Clean(filepath.FromSlash(path))
991		if clean == "." || strings.HasPrefix(clean, ".."+string(os.PathSeparator)) || clean == ".." {
992			return "", false
993		}
994		return clean, true

997	func sameFilesystemPath(a, b string) bool {
998		if a == "" || b == "" {
999			return false
1000		}
1001		absA, err := filepath.Abs(a)
1002		if err != nil {
1003			absA = filepath.Clean(a)
1004		}
1005		absB, err := filepath.Abs(b)
1006		if err != nil {
1007			absB = filepath.Clean(b)
1008		}
1009		if resolvedA, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(absA); err == nil {
1010			absA = resolvedA
1011		}
1012		if resolvedB, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(absB); err == nil {
1013			absB = resolvedB
1014		}
1015		return absA == absB
1016	}

1018	// Land performs the local land flow for a single submission.
1019	// It serializes primary-checkout git operations with the process-shared
1020	// main-git lock so separate ddx work processes cannot interleave landing with
1021	// tracker commits or pre-dispatch checkpoint/ref updates.
1022	//
1023	// projectRoot is the directory containing the project's .git. req.WorktreeDir,
1024	// when non-empty, is used as the git working directory for all commands; when
1025	// empty, projectRoot is used. Both usually refer to the same dir — the field
1026	// is kept for forward compatibility with multi-clone topologies.
1027	func Land(projectRoot string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps) (*LandResult, error) {
1028		var result *LandResult
1029		err := withMainGitLock(projectRoot, func() error {
1030			var landErr error
1031			result, landErr = landLocked(projectRoot, req, gitOps)
1032			return landErr
1033		})
1034		return result, err
1035	}
1036	
1037	func landLocked(projectRoot string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps) (*LandResult, error) {
1038		if gitOps == nil {
1039			gitOps = RealLandingGitOps{}
1040		}
1041		wd := req.WorktreeDir
1042		if wd == "" {
1043			wd = projectRoot
1044		}
1045	
1046		// Trivial guard — no commits to land.
1047		if req.ResultRev == "" || req.ResultRev == req.BaseRev {
1048			return &LandResult{Status: "no-changes", Reason: "result_rev == base_rev"}, nil
1049		}
1050		syncOperatorAfterLand := sameFilesystemPath(projectRoot, wd)
1051		var operatorDirtyBeforeLand []string
1052		if syncOperatorAfterLand {
1053			operatorDirtyBeforeLand = dirtyWorktreePaths(wd)
1054		}
1055	
1056		// Resolve target branch (default to project HEAD).
1057		targetBranch := req.TargetBranch
1058		if targetBranch == "" {
1059			br, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
1060			if err != nil {
1061				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target branch: %w", err)
1062			}
1063			targetBranch = br
1064		}
1065		if err := ensureLandingWorktreeReady(wd, targetBranch); err != nil {
1066			return nil, err
1067		}
1068		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
1069	
1070		currentTip, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, targetRef)
1071		if err != nil {
1072			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target tip %s: %w", targetRef, err)
1073		}
1074	
1075		contribCount := gitOps.CountCommits(wd, req.BaseRev, req.ResultRev)
1076	
1077		if preserved, err := preserveIfLargeDeletion(wd, req, gitOps, currentTip, contribCount); err != nil {
1078			return nil, err
1079		} else if preserved != nil {
1080			return preserved, nil
1081		}
1082		if preserved, err := preserveIfSyntaxSanityFails(wd, req, gitOps, currentTip, contribCount); err != nil {
1083			return nil, err
1084		} else if preserved != nil {
1085			return preserved, nil
1086		}
1087	
1088		// Fast path: no sibling advanced the target branch → straight ff via
1089		// update-ref. The worker's commit becomes the new tip unchanged; its
1090		// parent is still BaseRev, so replay sees the same inputs the worker
1091		// saw. No merge commit is created.
1092		if currentTip == req.BaseRev {
1093			if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, req.ResultRev, currentTip); err != nil {
1094				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, req.ResultRev, err)
1095			}
1096			result := &LandResult{
1097				Status:            "landed",
1098				NewTip:            req.ResultRev,
1099				Merged:            false,
1100				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
1101			}
1102			finalWD, cleanup, err := landingFinalizationWorktree(projectRoot, wd, targetBranch, gitOps)
1103			if err != nil {
1104				return nil, err
1105			}
1106			defer cleanup()
1107			if preserved, err := preserveIfPostLandGateFails(finalWD, req, gitOps, targetRef, currentTip, result.NewTip, contribCount, nil); err != nil {
1108				return nil, err
1109			} else if preserved != nil {
1110				return preserved, nil
1111			}
1112			if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
1113				if err := copyEvidenceDirForLanding(projectRoot, finalWD, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
1114					return nil, fmt.Errorf("copying evidence into landing worktree: %w", err)
1115				}
1116				if err := landEvidence(finalWD, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result); err != nil {
1117					return preserveAfterEvidenceFailure(finalWD, req, gitOps, targetRef, currentTip, result.NewTip, contribCount, nil, err)
1118				}
1119				cleanupProjectEvidenceCopy(projectRoot, req.EvidenceDir)
1120			}
1121			if syncOperatorAfterLand {
1122				syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps, wd, currentTip, operatorDirtyBeforeLand, result)
1123			}
1124			return result, nil
1125		}
1126	
1127		// Merge path: the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
1128		// a temp detached worktree at currentTip and run `git merge --no-ff
1129		// ResultRev` inside it. The result is a merge commit whose parents are
1130		// [currentTip, ResultRev]. Crucially, ResultRev itself is NOT rewritten:
1131		// its parent is still BaseRev, so replay observes the original inputs.
1132		tempWT, tempWtErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-land-wt-*")
1133		if tempWtErr != nil {
1134			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating temp worktree dir: %w", tempWtErr)
1135		}
1136		// os.MkdirTemp creates the dir, but git worktree add refuses to run if
1137		// the target already exists. Remove it first so git can recreate it.
1138		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
1139		if err := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, currentTip); err != nil {
1140			return nil, fmt.Errorf("adding temp worktree at %s: %w", currentTip, err)
1141		}
1142		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
1143	
1144		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge bead %s attempt %s into %s", req.BeadID, shortAttempt(req.AttemptID), targetBranch)
1145		if err := gitOps.MergeInto(tempWT, req.ResultRev, mergeMsg); err != nil {
1146			// Merge conflict: preserve the original ResultRev and return.
1147			preserveRef := landIterationRef(req.BeadID, req.AttemptID, currentTip)
1148			if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, preserveRef, req.ResultRev, ""); upErr != nil {
1149				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after merge conflict: %w", preserveRef, upErr)
1150			}
1151			return &LandResult{
1152				Status:            "preserved",
1153				PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
1154				Reason:            "merge conflict",
1155				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
1156			}, nil
1157		}
1158	
1159		// Merge clean: read the merge commit SHA from the temp worktree's HEAD
1160		// and fast-forward the target branch to it.
1161		mergeSHA, err := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
1162		if err != nil {
1163			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading merge HEAD: %w", err)
1164		}
1165		if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, currentTip); err != nil {
1166			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to merge commit %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, err)
1167		}
1168		result := &LandResult{
1169			Status:            "landed",
1170			NewTip:            mergeSHA,
1171			Merged:            true,
1172			MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
1173		}
1174		finalWD, cleanup, err := landingFinalizationWorktree(projectRoot, wd, targetBranch, gitOps)
1175		if err != nil {
1176			return nil, err
1177		}
1178		defer cleanup()
1179		if preserved, err := preserveIfPostLandGateFails(finalWD, req, gitOps, targetRef, currentTip, result.NewTip, contribCount, nil); err != nil {
1180			return nil, err
1181		} else if preserved != nil {
1182			return preserved, nil
1183		}
1184		if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
1185			if err := copyEvidenceDirForLanding(projectRoot, finalWD, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
1186				return nil, fmt.Errorf("copying evidence into landing worktree: %w", err)
1187			}
1188			if err := landEvidence(finalWD, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result); err != nil {
1189				return preserveAfterEvidenceFailure(finalWD, req, gitOps, targetRef, currentTip, result.NewTip, contribCount, nil, err)
1190			}
1191			cleanupProjectEvidenceCopy(projectRoot, req.EvidenceDir)
1192		}
1193		if syncOperatorAfterLand {
1194			syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps, wd, currentTip, operatorDirtyBeforeLand, result)
1195		}
1196		return result, nil
1197	}

1199	func preserveIfPostLandGateFails(wd string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, targetRef, preLandTip, landedTip string, contribCount int, dirtyBefore []string) (*LandResult, error) {
1200		if len(req.PostLandCommand) == 0 {
1201			return nil, nil
1202		}
1203		output, err := runPostLandCommand(wd, req.PostLandCommand)
1204		if err == nil {
1205			return nil, nil
1206		}
1207	
1208		preserveRef := landIterationRef(req.BeadID, req.AttemptID, preLandTip)
1209		if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, preserveRef, req.ResultRev, ""); upErr != nil {
1210			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after post-land gate: %w", preserveRef, upErr)
1211		}
1212		if revertErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, preLandTip, landedTip); revertErr != nil {
1213			return nil, fmt.Errorf("restoring %s to %s after post-land gate failed: %w", targetRef, preLandTip, revertErr)
1214		}
1215	
1216		reason := fmt.Sprintf("post-land gate failed: %s: %v", strings.Join(req.PostLandCommand, " "), err)
1217		if trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(output); trimmed != "" {
1218			reason += ": " + truncatePostLandGateOutput(trimmed)
1219		}
1220		result := &LandResult{
1221			Status:            "preserved",
1222			PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
1223			Reason:            reason,
1224			MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
1225		}
1226		syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps, wd, landedTip, dirtyBefore, result)
1227		return result, nil
1228	}

1230	func preserveAfterEvidenceFailure(wd string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, targetRef, preLandTip, landedTip string, contribCount int, dirtyBefore []string, evidenceErr error) (*LandResult, error) {
1231		preserveRef := landIterationRef(req.BeadID, req.AttemptID, preLandTip)
1232		if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, preserveRef, req.ResultRev, ""); upErr != nil {
1233			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after evidence commit failure: %w", preserveRef, upErr)
1234		}
1235		if landedTip != "" {
1236			if revertErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, preLandTip, landedTip); revertErr != nil {
1237				return nil, fmt.Errorf("restoring %s to %s after evidence commit failed: %w", targetRef, preLandTip, revertErr)
1238			}
1239		}
1240		result := &LandResult{
1241			Status:            "preserved",
1242			PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
1243			Reason:            "evidence commit failed: " + evidenceErr.Error(),
1244			MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
1245		}
1246		syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps, wd, landedTip, dirtyBefore, result)
1247		return result, nil
1248	}

1250	func runPostLandCommand(wd string, command []string) (string, error) {
1251		if len(command) == 0 {
1252			return "", nil
1253		}
1254		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Minute)
1255		defer cancel()
1256		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, command[0], command[1:]...)
1257		cmd.Dir = wd
1258		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
1259		if ctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {
1260			return string(out), ctx.Err()
1261		}
1262		return string(out), err
1263	}

1265	func truncatePostLandGateOutput(output string) string {
1266		const max = 2048
1267		if len(output) <= max {
1268			return output
1269		}
1270		return output[:max] + "...(truncated)"
1271	}

1273	type largeDeletionFinding struct {
1274		Path    string
1275		Deleted int
1276	}

1278	func preserveIfLargeDeletion(wd string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, currentTip string, contribCount int) (*LandResult, error) {
1279		threshold := largeDeletionLineThreshold(req)
1280		finding, found, err := largestDeletionFinding(gitOps, wd, req.BaseRev, req.ResultRev, threshold)
1281		if err != nil {
1282			return nil, err
1283		}
1284		if !found {
1285			return nil, nil
1286		}
1287		ack, err := landHasLargeDeletionAcknowledgement(wd, req.BaseRev, req.ResultRev)
1288		if err == nil && ack {
1289			return nil, nil
1290		}
1291	
1292		preserveRef := landIterationRef(req.BeadID, req.AttemptID, currentTip)
1293		if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, preserveRef, req.ResultRev, ""); upErr != nil {
1294			return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after large-deletion gate: %w", preserveRef, upErr)
1295		}
1296		return &LandResult{
1297			Status:            "preserved",
1298			PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
1299			Reason:            fmt.Sprintf("large-deletion gate: %s deleted %d lines (threshold %d) without intentional large deletion acknowledgement", finding.Path, finding.Deleted, threshold),
1300			MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
1301		}, nil
1302	}
1303	
1304	func largeDeletionLineThreshold(req LandRequest) int {
1305		if req.LargeDeletionLineThreshold > 0 {
1306			return req.LargeDeletionLineThreshold
1307		}
1308		return defaultLargeDeletionLineThreshold
1309	}

1311	func largestDeletionFinding(gitOps LandingGitOps, wd, baseRev, resultRev string, threshold int) (largeDeletionFinding, bool, error) {
1312		raw, err := gitOps.DiffNumstat(wd, baseRev, resultRev)
1313		if err != nil {
1314			return largeDeletionFinding{}, false, fmt.Errorf("checking large deletions: %w", err)
1315		}
1316		var largest largeDeletionFinding
1317		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(raw), "\n") {
1318			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
1319				continue
1320			}
1321			parts := strings.SplitN(line, "\t", 3)
1322			if len(parts) != 3 || parts[1] == "-" {
1323				continue
1324			}
1325			deleted, parseErr := strconv.Atoi(parts[1])
1326			if parseErr != nil {
1327				continue
1328			}
1329			if deleted > threshold && deleted > largest.Deleted {
1330				largest = largeDeletionFinding{Path: parts[2], Deleted: deleted}
1331			}
1332		}
1333		return largest, largest.Path != "", nil
1334	}

1336	func landHasLargeDeletionAcknowledgement(wd, baseRev, resultRev string) (bool, error) {
1337		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wd, "log", "--format=%B", baseRev+".."+resultRev).CombinedOutput()
1338		if err != nil {
1339			return false, fmt.Errorf("checking large-deletion acknowledgement: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
1340		}
1341		msg := strings.ToLower(string(out))
1342		for _, marker := range []string{
1343			"intentional large deletion",
1344			"intentional file removal",
1345			"intentional file deletion",
1346		} {
1347			if strings.Contains(msg, marker) {
1348				return true, nil
1349			}
1350		}
1351		return false, nil
1352	}

1380	func syntaxSanityFindingForResult(gitOps LandingGitOps, wd, baseRev, resultRev string) (syntaxSanityFinding, bool, error) {
1381		paths, err := gitOps.DiffNameOnly(wd, baseRev, resultRev)
1382		if err != nil {
1383			return syntaxSanityFinding{}, false, err
1384		}
1385		for _, path := range paths {
1386			result, ok, err := gitFileAt(wd, resultRev, path)
1387			if err != nil {
1388				return syntaxSanityFinding{}, false, err
1389			}
1390			if !ok {
1391				continue // deleted files are handled by deletion gates, not syntax checks.
1392			}
1393			base, _, err := gitFileAt(wd, baseRev, path)
1394			if err != nil {
1395				return syntaxSanityFinding{}, false, err
1396			}
1397			if reason, bad := syntaxSanityFailure(path, base, result); bad {
1398				return syntaxSanityFinding{Path: path, Reason: reason}, true, nil
1399			}
1400		}
1401		return syntaxSanityFinding{}, false, nil
1402	}

1404	func gitFileAt(wd, rev, path string) ([]byte, bool, error) {
1405		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wd, "show", rev+":"+path).CombinedOutput()
1406		if err != nil {
1407			if strings.Contains(string(out), "does not exist") || strings.Contains(string(out), "exists on disk, but not in") {
1408				return nil, false, nil
1409			}
1410			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("reading %s at %s: %s: %w", path, rev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
1411		}
1412		return out, true, nil
1413	}

1415	func syntaxSanityFailure(path string, base, result []byte) (string, bool) {
1416		switch strings.ToLower(filepath.Ext(path)) {
1417		case ".json":
1418			var v any
1419			if err := json.Unmarshal(result, &v); err != nil {
1420				return "invalid JSON: " + err.Error(), true
1421			}
1422		case ".go":
1423			if _, err := parser.ParseFile(token.NewFileSet(), path, result, parser.AllErrors); err != nil {
1424				return "invalid Go syntax: " + err.Error(), true
1425			}
1426		case ".svelte":
1427			return svelteSanityFailure(base, result)
1428		}
1429		return "", false
1430	}
1431	
1432	func svelteSanityFailure(base, result []byte) (string, bool) {
1433		baseHadScript := bytes.Contains(base, []byte("<script"))
1434		resultHasScript := bytes.Contains(result, []byte("<script"))
1435		resultClosesScript := bytes.Contains(result, []byte("</script>"))
1436		if baseHadScript && (!resultHasScript || !resultClosesScript) {
1437			return "Svelte file lost script structure", true
1438		}
1439		if resultHasScript && !resultClosesScript {
1440			return "Svelte script tag is not closed", true
1441		}
1442		return "", false
1443	}

1445	// LandConflictAutoRecover attempts a 3-way merge of a preserved iteration ref
1446	// onto the current target-branch tip using the ort strategy with -X ours. The
1447	// strategy resolves mechanical conflicts (positional drift from parallel beads)
1448	// by favouring the current tip's version of any conflicting sections while still
1449	// including the preserved iteration's non-conflicting changes in the merge
1450	// commit. If the merge succeeds the local target branch is advanced and the new
1451	// merge commit SHA is returned.
1452	//
1453	// A non-nil error means the ort merge failed (unresolvable content conflict or
1454	// git error) — the caller should escalate to a focused conflict-resolve agent.
1455	// The target branch is never modified on error.
1456	func LandConflictAutoRecover(wd, preserveRef string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (string, error) {
1457		dirtyBefore := dirtyWorktreePaths(wd)
1458		targetBranch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
1459		if err != nil {
1460			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving target branch: %w", err)
1461		}
1462		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
1463	
1464		currentTip, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, targetRef)
1465		if err != nil {
1466			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving target tip %s: %w", targetRef, err)
1467		}
1468	
1469		iterSHA, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, preserveRef)
1470		if err != nil {
1471			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving preserved ref %s: %w", preserveRef, err)
1472		}
1473	
1474		tempWT, mkErr := os.MkdirTemp("", "ddx-conflict-recover-*")
1475		if mkErr != nil {
1476			return "", fmt.Errorf("creating temp worktree: %w", mkErr)
1477		}
1478		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
1479		if addErr := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, currentTip); addErr != nil {
1480			return "", fmt.Errorf("adding temp worktree at %s: %w", currentTip, addErr)
1481		}
1482		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
1483	
1484		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge preserved iteration %s after base drift (ort -X ours)", preserveRef)
1485		out, mergeErr := internalgit.Command(
1486			context.Background(), tempWT,
1487			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
1488			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",

1501			return "", fmt.Errorf("advancing %s to %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, updErr)

1507	// shortAttempt returns a 10-char slug derived from attemptID for use in temp
1508	// branch names. When attemptID is empty, it returns the current timestamp.
1509	func shortAttempt(attemptID string) string {
1510		if attemptID != "" {
1511			if len(attemptID) > 16 {
1512				return attemptID[:16]
1513			}
1514			return attemptID
1515		}
1516		return NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405")
1517	}

1519	// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult maps a LandResult onto an
1520	// ExecuteBeadResult so the execute-bead loop sees the correct final status.
1521	// It is the coordinator-pattern replacement for ApplyLandingToResult.
1522	func ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, land *LandResult) {
1523		if land == nil || res == nil {
1524			return
1525		}
1526		switch land.Status {
1527		case "landed":
1528			// Fast-forward or merge commit — either way the target branch now
1529			// contains the worker's result. ResultRev's own parent is still
1530			// BaseRev so replay fidelity is preserved.
1531			res.Outcome = "merged"
1532			res.Reason = ""
1533			res.PreserveRef = ""
1534			if land.Merged {
1535				res.Reason = "merged onto current tip"
1536			}
1537			if land.CheckoutSyncDeferred {
1538				detail := "checkout_sync_deferred"
1539				if len(land.CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths) > 0 {
1540					detail += ": " + strings.Join(land.CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths, ", ")
1541				}
1542				if res.Reason == "" {
1543					res.Reason = detail
1544				} else {
1545					res.Reason += "; " + detail
1546				}
1547			}
1548			// NewTip reflects the ref actually on the target branch (either
1549			// ResultRev on the ff path or the merge commit SHA on the merge path).
1550			if land.NewTip != "" {
1551				// Preserve the implementation rev before rewriting the compat alias.
1552				if res.ImplementationRev == "" {
1553					res.ImplementationRev = res.ResultRev
1554				}
1555				res.LandedRev = land.NewTip
1556				res.ResultRev = land.NewTip // backwards-compat alias mirrors LandedRev
1557			}
1558		case "preserved":
1559			res.Outcome = "preserved"
1560			res.Reason = land.Reason
1561			res.PreserveRef = land.PreserveRef
1562		case "no-changes":
1563			// Only overwrite when the worker itself did not already report
1564			// a richer no-changes rationale.
1565			if res.Outcome == "" || res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded {
1566				res.Outcome = "no-changes"
1567			}
1568			if res.Reason == "" {
1569				res.Reason = land.Reason
1570			}
1571		}
1572		// Re-classify loop-visible status from the landing outcome.
1573		reasonForStatus := res.Reason
1574		if land.Status == "preserved" {
1575			// Route preserve reasons through the land-conflict classifier so the
1576			// loop sees land_conflict (not generic success).
1577			if strings.HasPrefix(res.Reason, PreMergeChecksReason) ||
1578				strings.HasPrefix(res.Reason, "evidence commit failed:") {
1579				reasonForStatus = res.Reason
1580			} else {
1581				reasonForStatus = "merge conflict"
1582			}
1583		}
1584		res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, reasonForStatus)
1585		res.OrchestratorStatus = res.Status
1586		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
1587	}
1588	
1589	// BuildLandRequestFromResult constructs a LandRequest for the coordinator from
1590	// an ExecuteBeadResult. The coordinator always passes projectRoot as the
1591	// workdir — the worker's original worktree has already been cleaned up by the
1592	// time Land() runs.
1593	func BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult) LandRequest {
1594		// Use the pre-landing implementation revision. If ImplementationRev is set
1595		// (i.e. the result was already landed once and ResultRev was rewritten to
1596		// the branch tip), prefer it so the coordinator always sees the candidate
1597		// commit rather than an already-landed or evidence rev.
1598		candidateRev := res.ImplementationRev
1599		if candidateRev == "" {
1600			candidateRev = res.ResultRev
1601		}
1602		return LandRequest{
1603			WorktreeDir:  projectRoot,
1604			BaseRev:      res.BaseRev,
1605			ResultRev:    candidateRev,
1606			BeadID:       res.BeadID,
1607			AttemptID:    res.AttemptID,
1608			TargetBranch: "",
1609			EvidenceDir:  res.ExecutionDir,
1610		}
1611	}

16	// gitIndexLockStaleAge is the age after which a `.git/index.lock` with no
17	// identifiable live owner is considered stale and safe to remove.
18	//
19	// All DDx writers serialize through withMainGitLock (.ddx/.git-tracker.lock),
20	// so an unowned index.lock past this threshold represents either a crashed
21	// git process or an operator command that exited without cleanup.
22	var gitIndexLockStaleAge = 30 * time.Second
23	
24	// gitIndexLockRecoveryAttempts bounds the total number of attempts
25	// (including the initial try) made by runGitWithIndexLockRecovery.
26	const gitIndexLockRecoveryAttempts = 3
27	
28	// gitIndexLockLiveOwnerWait is the wait between retries when a live process
29	// is holding the lock. Short by design: stalling is not acceptable.
30	var gitIndexLockLiveOwnerWait = 500 * time.Millisecond
31	
32	// isGitIndexLockError reports whether the git output is the
33	// `.git/index.lock`-exists error.
34	func isGitIndexLockError(output string) bool {
35		lower := strings.ToLower(output)
36		return strings.Contains(lower, "index.lock") &&
37			(strings.Contains(lower, "file exists") || strings.Contains(lower, "another git process"))
38	}
39	
40	// gitIndexLockPath returns the absolute path to .git/index.lock for
41	// projectRoot. The caller is responsible for ensuring projectRoot is a
42	// real worktree (the lock-recovery code handles missing files).
43	func gitIndexLockPath(projectRoot string) string {
44		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".git", "index.lock")
45	}
46	
47	// gitIndexLockOwner attempts to identify the pid that currently holds
48	// lockPath open. Uses lsof if available on PATH. Returns (0, nil) if no
49	// owner can be determined (lsof missing, no process has it open, or
50	// lsof errored). Failure to identify is not itself an error.
51	func gitIndexLockOwner(lockPath string) (int, error) {
52		lsof, err := exec.LookPath("lsof")
53		if err != nil {
54			return 0, nil
55		}
56		// lsof -t writes one pid per line for each process with the file open.
57		out, err := exec.Command(lsof, "-t", "--", lockPath).Output()
58		if err != nil {
59			// lsof exits non-zero when no process has the file open, which is
60			// the common case we want to treat as "no owner found".
61			return 0, nil
62		}
63		s := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
64		if s == "" {
65			return 0, nil
66		}
67		first := strings.SplitN(s, "\n", 2)[0]
68		pid, perr := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(first))
69		if perr != nil {
70			return 0, nil
71		}
72		return pid, nil
73	}

75	// gitIndexLockRecoveryResult describes the outcome of one recovery attempt
76	// against `.git/index.lock`.
77	type gitIndexLockRecoveryResult struct {
78		// Removed is true if the lock file was deleted.
79		Removed bool
80		// OwnerPID is the pid identified as holding the lock, or 0 if unknown.
81		OwnerPID int
82		// OwnerAlive reports the liveness of OwnerPID at inspection time.
83		// Meaningful only when OwnerPID != 0.
84		OwnerAlive bool
85		// Age is the lock-file age at inspection (zero if lock was missing).
86		Age time.Duration
87		// Reason is a human-readable summary suitable for log output.
88		Reason string

91	// recoverGitIndexLock inspects `.git/index.lock` under projectRoot. If the
92	// lock has no live owner — either lsof identifies a dead pid, or no pid
93	// holds it and the mtime is past gitIndexLockStaleAge — the lock is
94	// removed. Otherwise the lock is left in place and the result describes
95	// the live owner.
96	func recoverGitIndexLock(projectRoot string) (gitIndexLockRecoveryResult, error) {
97		lockPath := gitIndexLockPath(projectRoot)
98		info, statErr := os.Lstat(lockPath)
99		if statErr != nil {
100			if os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
101				return gitIndexLockRecoveryResult{
102					Reason: "lock not present",
103				}, nil
104			}
105			return gitIndexLockRecoveryResult{}, fmt.Errorf("stat %s: %w", lockPath, statErr)
106		}
107		age := time.Since(info.ModTime())
108		pid, _ := gitIndexLockOwner(lockPath)
109	
110		if pid > 0 {
111			alive := trackerProcessAlive(pid)
112			if !alive {
113				if rmErr := os.Remove(lockPath); rmErr != nil && !os.IsNotExist(rmErr) {
114					return gitIndexLockRecoveryResult{
115						OwnerPID: pid, Age: age,
116					}, fmt.Errorf("remove lock owned by dead pid %d: %w", pid, rmErr)
117				}
118				return gitIndexLockRecoveryResult{
119					Removed: true, OwnerPID: pid, OwnerAlive: false, Age: age,
120					Reason: fmt.Sprintf("removed: owner pid %d not alive (lock age %s)", pid, age.Round(time.Second)),
121				}, nil
122			}
123			return gitIndexLockRecoveryResult{
124				OwnerPID: pid, OwnerAlive: true, Age: age,
125				Reason: fmt.Sprintf("live owner pid %d (lock age %s)", pid, age.Round(time.Second)),
126			}, nil
127		}
128	
129		if age >= gitIndexLockStaleAge {
130			if rmErr := os.Remove(lockPath); rmErr != nil && !os.IsNotExist(rmErr) {
131				return gitIndexLockRecoveryResult{Age: age}, fmt.Errorf("remove unowned stale lock (age %s): %w", age, rmErr)
132			}
133			return gitIndexLockRecoveryResult{
134				Removed: true, Age: age,
135				Reason: fmt.Sprintf("removed: no owner found, age %s >= stale threshold %s", age.Round(time.Second), gitIndexLockStaleAge),
136			}, nil
137		}
138	
139		return gitIndexLockRecoveryResult{
140			Age:    age,
141			Reason: fmt.Sprintf("no owner found but lock is fresh (age %s < %s)", age.Round(time.Second), gitIndexLockStaleAge),
142		}, nil
143	}

145	// runGitWithIndexLockRecovery runs `git args...` in dir and, on
146	// `.git/index.lock` contention, identifies the owner and either removes
147	// a stale lock or briefly waits for a live owner before retrying.
148	//
149	// Total attempts are bounded by gitIndexLockRecoveryAttempts; the final
150	// error is decorated with the most recent recovery diagnostic. The
151	// helper deliberately does NOT loop for tens of seconds — stalling is
152	// not acceptable here.
153	func runGitWithIndexLockRecovery(ctx context.Context, dir string, args ...string) ([]byte, error) {
154		var lastOut []byte
155		var lastErr error
156		var lastDiag string
157		for attempt := 0; attempt < gitIndexLockRecoveryAttempts; attempt++ {
158			out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
159			if err == nil {
160				return out, nil
161			}
162			if !isGitIndexLockError(string(out)) {
163				return out, err
164			}
165			lastOut = out
166			lastErr = err
167			result, recErr := recoverGitIndexLock(dir)
168			if recErr != nil {
169				return out, fmt.Errorf("%s; index-lock recovery failed: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), recErr)
170			}
171			lastDiag = result.Reason
172			if !result.Removed {
173				time.Sleep(gitIndexLockLiveOwnerWait)
174			}
175		}
176		if lastDiag == "" {
177			return lastOut, lastErr
178		}
179		return lastOut, fmt.Errorf("%s; index-lock owner: %s: %w",
180			strings.TrimSpace(string(lastOut)), lastDiag, lastErr)
181	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"os"
6		"path/filepath"
7		"strconv"
8		"strings"
9		"time"
10	)
11	
12	// LockRetryPolicy describes the retry/backoff curve used when attempting to
13	// acquire a process-shared lock that guards git index operations on the
14	// primary .git (the tracker-commit lock). The policy is owned by this
15	// package per ddx-da11a34a AC#4: callers in cli/internal/triage and
16	// elsewhere consult ActionRetryWithBackoff but do not duplicate the curve
17	// implementation here.
18	//
19	// The curve is exponential with a cap and a bounded max-retries budget so
20	// that lock contention is absorbed silently up to a point, after which it
21	// surfaces as an ordinary error to the caller. Per TD-031 §8.5
22	// (LockContentionContract) lock contention does NOT change persisted bead
23	// status; the bead remains claimable and the worker remains `draining`.
24	type LockRetryPolicy struct {
25		// InitialBackoff is the wait before the second attempt.
26		InitialBackoff time.Duration
27		// MaxBackoff caps the per-step backoff after exponential growth.
28		MaxBackoff time.Duration
29		// Multiplier is the per-step growth factor (>= 1.0).
30		Multiplier float64
31		// MaxRetries bounds the number of acquisition attempts after the
32		// initial try. Total attempts = MaxRetries + 1.
33		MaxRetries int
34		// MaxElapsed bounds the total wall-clock time spent retrying. If both
35		// MaxRetries and MaxElapsed are set, the first to trip wins.
36		MaxElapsed time.Duration
37	}
38	
39	// DefaultLockRetryPolicy returns the policy used by withTrackerLock. The
40	// curve is intentionally tight (sub-second) for in-process tracker-commit
41	// contention; the larger 30s/90s/300s curve referenced by the auto-triage
42	// child of ddx-3c154349 lives one layer up, in cli/internal/triage —
43	// it consults ActionRetryWithBackoff and re-queues across drain passes,
44	// while this policy resolves contention within a single attempt.
45	func DefaultLockRetryPolicy() LockRetryPolicy {
46		return LockRetryPolicy{
47			InitialBackoff: 50 * time.Millisecond,
48			MaxBackoff:     1 * time.Second,
49			Multiplier:     2.0,
50			MaxRetries:     200,
51			MaxElapsed:     30 * time.Second,
52		}
53	}
54	
55	// step returns the backoff duration for retry index n (0-based: n=0 is the
56	// wait BEFORE the second acquisition attempt).
57	func (p LockRetryPolicy) step(n int) time.Duration {
58		if p.InitialBackoff <= 0 {
59			return 0
60		}
61		d := float64(p.InitialBackoff)
62		m := p.Multiplier
63		if m < 1 {
64			m = 1
65		}
66		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
67			d *= m
68			if p.MaxBackoff > 0 && d >= float64(p.MaxBackoff) {
69				d = float64(p.MaxBackoff)
70				break
71			}
72		}
73		out := time.Duration(d)
74		if p.MaxBackoff > 0 && out > p.MaxBackoff {
75			out = p.MaxBackoff
76		}
77		return out
78	}
79	
80	// trackerLockStaleAge is the age after which a held tracker lock is
81	// considered stale (owner crashed mid-commit) and may be forcibly broken.
82	var trackerLockStaleAge = 10 * time.Minute
83	
84	// trackerLockPolicy is the active retry policy for withTrackerLock.
85	// Tests may swap this out via the exported helpers below.
86	var trackerLockPolicy = DefaultLockRetryPolicy()
87	
88	// trackerLockPath returns the main-git lock directory for the given project
89	// root. The historical name remains because tests and older comments refer to
90	// the tracker lock, but the lock now protects all primary-checkout git writes:
91	// tracker commits, pre-dispatch checkpoint/ref updates, and landing.
92	func trackerLockPath(projectRoot string) string {
93		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ".git-tracker.lock")
94	}
95	
96	// withMainGitLock acquires the process-shared main-git lock for the
97	// given project root, runs fn, and releases the lock. The lock is a
98	// directory created via os.Mkdir (atomic across processes on POSIX and
99	// Windows) following the same pattern as cli/internal/bead/lock.go.
100	//
101	// Contention is handled per LockRetryPolicy: exponential backoff up to a
102	// bounded budget. Per TD-031 §8.5 the contention itself does not change
103	// bead status; on budget exhaustion the caller surfaces an ordinary
104	// execution_failed and triage decides next steps.
105	func withMainGitLock(projectRoot string, fn func() error) error {
106		if projectRoot == "" {
107			return fn()
108		}
109		return withTrackerLockPolicy(projectRoot, trackerLockPolicy, fn)
110	}
111	
112	// withTrackerLock is retained for existing tracker/pre-dispatch call sites. It
113	// uses the same process-shared lock as landing so separate ddx work processes
114	// cannot interleave commits, ref updates, and checkout sync in the main tree.
115	func withTrackerLock(projectRoot string, fn func() error) error {
116		return withMainGitLock(projectRoot, fn)
117	}
118	
119	// TrackerLockSample carries timing and contention metrics for one
120	// withTrackerLock / withMainGitLock acquire+release cycle.
121	type TrackerLockSample struct {
122		LockDir string
123		Wait    time.Duration // time from entry to lock acquisition
124		Hold    time.Duration // time the callback held the lock
125		Retries int           // number of sleep-and-retry iterations (0 = acquired on first try)
126	}
127	
128	// TrackerLockMetricsSink is called after each successful tracker-lock
129	// acquire+release cycle. The zero value (nil) is a no-op. Tests may swap
130	// this to capture metrics.
131	var TrackerLockMetricsSink func(TrackerLockSample)
132	
133	// withTrackerLockPolicy is the policy-parameterised form of
134	// withTrackerLock; exposed at package scope so tests can pin a specific
135	// curve.
136	func withTrackerLockPolicy(projectRoot string, policy LockRetryPolicy, fn func() error) error {
137		lockDir := trackerLockPath(projectRoot)
138		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(lockDir), 0o755); err != nil {
139			return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock dir: %w", err)
140		}
141	
142		start := time.Now()
143		retries := 0
144		for attempt := 0; ; attempt++ {
145			err := os.Mkdir(lockDir, 0o755)
146			if err == nil {
147				_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid"),
148					[]byte(fmt.Sprintf("%d", os.Getpid())), 0o644)
149				_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "acquired_at"),
150					[]byte(time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)), 0o644)
151				break
152			}
153	
154			// Classify what exists at lockDir before deciding to sleep or fail.
155			// Per TD-031 §8.5: only a confirmed lock directory enters retry/backoff;
156			// malformed paths are operator diagnostics, not lock contention.
157			info, statErr := os.Lstat(lockDir)
158			if statErr != nil {
159				if os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
160					// Path disappeared between Mkdir and Lstat (race). Retry immediately.
161					continue
162				}
163				return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock stat: %w", statErr)
164			}
165	
166			switch {
167			case info.Mode().IsDir():
168				// Real lock directory. Apply existing stale-owner policy.
169				if breakStaleTrackerLock(lockDir) {
170					continue
171				}
172				if attempt >= policy.MaxRetries {
173					return lockTimeoutError(lockDir, "max retries")
174				}
175				if policy.MaxElapsed > 0 && time.Since(start) >= policy.MaxElapsed {
176					return lockTimeoutError(lockDir, "max elapsed")
177				}
178				retries++
179				time.Sleep(policy.step(attempt))
180	
181			case info.Mode().IsRegular():
182				// Malformed: lock path must be a directory, not a regular file.
183				if time.Since(info.ModTime()) > trackerLockStaleAge {
184					// Stale regular file: remove with single-path removal and retry immediately.
185					if rmErr := os.Remove(lockDir); rmErr != nil && !os.IsNotExist(rmErr) {
186						return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock: remove malformed stale file %s: %w", lockDir, rmErr)
187					}
188					continue
189				}
190				return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock: malformed lock path %s is a regular file; a lock directory was expected — remove it manually to recover", lockDir)
191	
192			default:
193				// Symlink, socket, device, or other special filesystem object. Do not remove.
194				return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock: malformed lock path %s has type %v; a lock directory was expected — inspect and remove it manually to recover", lockDir, info.Mode().Type())
195			}
196		}
197	
198		waitDur := time.Since(start)
199		holdStart := time.Now()
200		defer os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
201		fnErr := fn()
202		if sink := TrackerLockMetricsSink; sink != nil {
203			sink(TrackerLockSample{LockDir: lockDir, Wait: waitDur, Hold: time.Since(holdStart), Retries: retries})
204		}
205		return fnErr
206	}
207	
208	func lockTimeoutError(lockDir, why string) error {
209		owner := "missing"
210		if pidData, perr := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid")); perr == nil && len(pidData) > 0 {
211			owner = strings.TrimSpace(string(pidData))
212		}
213		return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock timeout (%s, lock: %s, owner pid: %s)", why, lockDir, owner)
214	}
215	
216	// breakStaleTrackerLock removes lockDir if its owner process is dead or the
217	// lock is older than trackerLockStaleAge. Returns true if the lock was broken.
218	func breakStaleTrackerLock(lockDir string) bool {
219		pidData, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid"))
220		if err == nil {
221			pid, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(string(pidData)))
222			if err == nil && pid > 0 && pid != os.Getpid() {
223				if !trackerProcessAlive(pid) {
224					os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
225					return true
226				}
227			}
228		}
229	
230		acquiredData, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "acquired_at"))
231		if err == nil {
232			acquired, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, strings.TrimSpace(string(acquiredData)))
233			if err == nil && time.Since(acquired) > trackerLockStaleAge {
234				os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
235				return true
236			}
237		}
238	
239		return false
240	}
